perturbation.original_text: Each time VS is requested to perform Services, PPD shall provide VS with a work order or other similar written document mutually agreed upon by VS and PPD which shall set forth the specific services being requested, the compensation therefore and such other terms, conditions and specifications as VS and PPD may mutually agree (referred to herein as a "Work Order"). The parties acknowledge that no services shall be conducted by VS and no compensation shall be due and payable to VS without a Work Order. In the event PPD receives a change order from a Sponsor which includes changes to VS's Services as set forth in an executed Work Order or may otherwise impact the provision by VS of Services to PPD hereunder, PPD shall promptly notify VS. Thereafter, the parties shall negotiate, in good faith, for an amendment to the applicable Work Order, as appropriate, as a result of such Sponsor change order.
perturbation.changed_text: Each time VS is requested to perform Services, PPD may provide VS with a document which shall set forth the services being requested and the compensation therefore (referred to herein as a "Work Order"). In the event PPD receives a change order from a Sponsor which includes changes to VS's Services as set forth in an executed Work Order or may otherwise impact the provision by VS of Services to PPD hereunder, PPD shall promptly notify VS. Thereafter, the parties shall negotiate for an amendment to the applicable Work Order, as appropriate, as a result of such Sponsor change order.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'or other similar written document mutually agreed upon by VS and PPD' and 'such other terms, conditions and specifications as VS and PPD may mutually agree' significantly weakens the Work Order definition. Also by omitting 'The parties acknowledge that no services shall be conducted by VS and no compensation shall be due and payable to VS without a Work Order' creates an In-Text Contradiction because it creates a situation where services could be asked outside of the work order, which can create confusion in Section 4 (Compensation), where services and compensation are discussed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: VS shall be compensated for the Services in accordance with the Work Order. All pricing and rates used to establish the budget for Services set forth in each Work Order shall be in accordance with the pricing structures agreed upon by the parties herein and set forth in Exhibit B attached hereto and incorporated herein by reference. PPD acknowledges that VS customarily receives an advance payment in connection with the provision of imaging services to its clients. PPD agrees that, where appropriate and permitted by the Sponsor, PPD will provide for an advance payment mutually determined by PPD and VS (and acceptable to Sponsor) in connection with the provision of Preferred Services to such Sponsor.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated a mutually agreed upon written work order for services and compensation. It also states that no services shall be conducted without a work order, and no compensation shall be due. The changed text weakens this requirement by stating that PPD 'may' provide a document setting forth the services and compensation, removing the mutual agreement aspect and the explicit requirement for a work order before services and compensation. This contradicts the contradicted text, which states that 'VS shall be compensated for the Services in accordance with the Work Order,' because now services might be rendered without a work order, causing disputes regarding compensation. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists because the changed text undermines the work order requirement that the compensation clause relies upon.
